name: worktree-manager-skill
description: Comprehensive git worktree management. Use when the user wants to create, remove, list, or manage worktrees. Handles all worktree operations including creation, deletion, and status checking.
capabilities: Create git worktrees with automatic port allocation
List all worktrees with status and service information
Remove worktrees with automatic cleanup of services
Handle port conflicts by calculating unique offsets
Manage background processes for server and client
Set up isolated configuration for each worktree
author: EGAdams
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files that provide guidance for git worktree operations. No executable code exists. All 123 static findings are false positives caused by pattern matching on documentation text that describes slash commands and configuration examples.
valueStatement: Managing multiple development branches simultaneously is complex and error-prone. This skill provides slash commands to safely create, list, and remove git worktrees with automatic port allocation and service management.
userTitle: Manage Git Worktrees for Parallel Development
limitations: Only works with git repositories that have worktree support enabled
Requires existing branches to create worktrees from
Cannot manage worktrees outside the trees directory convention
Depends on Claude Code slash commands being available

useCases.title: Parallel Feature Work
useCases.description: Work on multiple features simultaneously without stashing or switching branches

useCases.title: Review PRs in Isolation
useCases.description: Test pull requests in isolated environments before merging

useCases.title: Hotfix While Developing
useCases.description: Apply production fixes while continuing feature development

promptTemplates.title: Create Worktree
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a worktree for the [branch-name] branch

promptTemplates.title: List Worktrees
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me my current worktrees and their status

promptTemplates.title: Remove Worktree
promptTemplates.prompt: Remove the [branch-name] worktree, I am done with it

promptTemplates.title: Parallel Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create worktrees for feature-a, feature-b, and feature-c so I can work on all three

bestPractices: Remove worktrees promptly after merging to free up ports and disk space
Use descriptive branch names that match your git workflow convention
List worktrees regularly to identify abandoned or stuck environments
antiPatterns: Manually editing worktree files instead of using slash commands
Creating worktrees for quick one-line changes when git checkout suffices
Leaving worktrees running when not actively developing on them

faq.question: What tools is this skill compatible with?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Requires slash command support.

faq.question: How many worktrees can I create?
faq.answer: Limited by available ports and disk space. Port offsets support dozens of worktrees.

faq.question: Does this integrate with existing git workflows?
faq.answer: Yes, uses standard git worktree commands and fits any git-based workflow.

faq.question: Is my data safe in worktrees?
faq.answer: Worktrees share git history but have isolated file systems and configurations.

faq.question: Why are my services not starting?
faq.answer: Check port conflicts with /list_worktrees or recreate the worktree if processes crashed.

faq.question: How is this different from git checkout?
faq.answer: Worktrees allow multiple branches simultaneously without switching or stashing changes.
